items.q: What determines whether a timber is a hardwood or softwood?
items.correct: The type of tree - deciduous (hardwood) or coniferous (softwood), not its hardness.
items.misconception: Whether the wood itself is physically hard or soft.
items.random_wrong: How long the tree took to grow.
items.why: Students assume 'hardwood' means hard. Balsa is a hardwood but very soft; yew is a softwood but harder than many hardwoods. Classification is BOTANICAL, not based on hardness.

items.q: Why is injection moulding suited to high-volume production?
items.correct: Tooling cost is high but per-unit cost is very low at scale.
items.misconception: It is the cheapest method for any quantity.
items.random_wrong: It is the only way to make plastic items.
items.why: Students think a 'good' process is good for everything. Injection moulding has expensive moulds - it's only economical when costs are spread over thousands or millions of parts.

items.q: What gives carbon fibre composite its strength?
items.correct: The fibres provide tensile strength; the resin matrix holds them in place and transfers loads.
items.misconception: The carbon fibres alone - the resin is just glue.
items.why: Students dismiss the matrix as filler. In reality the matrix is essential: it transfers loads between fibres and protects them. Loose carbon fibres without matrix have very little structural strength.

items.q: Which joint uses a peg inserted into a matching slot?
items.why: It's Mortise and tenon. “Butt joint” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
It's Remove thin shavings. “Drill round holes” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: If 6 dowels are used per cabinet and you make 25 cabinets, how many dowels are needed?
items.why: Exact answer: 150. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
